verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Eliphaz Themanites, dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭יַּעַן אֱלִיפַ֥ז הַֽתֵּימָנִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.king_james: Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite, and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid sapiens respondebit quasi in ventum loquens,  et implebit ardore stomachum suum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶחָכָ֗ם יַעֲנֶ֥ה דַעַת־ר֑וּחַ וִימַלֵּ֖א קָדִ֣ים בִּטְנֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Should a wise man utter vain knowledge, and fill his belly with the east wind?

verses.latin_vulgate: Arguis verbis eum qui non est æqualis tibi,  et loqueris quod tibi non expedit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הוֹכֵ֣חַ בְּ֭דָבָר לֹ֣א יִסְכּ֑וֹן וּ֝מִלִּ֗ים לֹא־יוֹעִ֥יל בָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Should he reason with unprofitable talk? or with speeches wherewith he can do no good?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quantum in te est, evacuasti timorem,  et tulisti preces coram Deo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַף־אַ֭תָּה תָּפֵ֣ר יִרְאָ֑ה וְתִגְרַ֥ע שִׂ֝יחָ֗ה לִפְנֵי־אֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Yea, thou castest off fear, and restrainest prayer before God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Docuit enim iniquitas tua os tuum,  et imitaris linguam blasphemantium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י יְאַלֵּ֣ף עֲוֺנְךָ֣ פִ֑יךָ וְ֝תִבְחַ֗ר לְשׁ֣וֹן עֲרוּמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Condemnabit te os tuum, et non ego :  et labia tua respondebunt tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַרְשִׁיעֲךָ֣ פִ֣יךָ וְלֹא־אָ֑נִי וּ֝שְׂפָתֶ֗יךָ יַעֲנוּ־בָֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea, thine own lips testify against thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid primus homo tu natus es,  et ante colles formatus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲרִאישׁ֣וֹן אָ֭דָם תִּוָּלֵ֑ד וְלִפְנֵ֖י גְבָע֣וֹת חוֹלָֽלְתָּ׃
verses.king_james: Art thou the first man that was born? or wast thou made before the hills?

verses.latin_vulgate: numquid consilium Dei audisti,  et inferior te erit ejus sapientia ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַבְס֣וֹד אֱל֣וֹהַּ תִּשְׁמָ֑ע וְתִגְרַ֖ע אֵלֶ֣יךָ חׇכְמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Hast thou heard the secret of God? and dost thou restrain wisdom to thyself?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid nosti quod ignoremus ?  quid intelligis quod nesciamus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־יָּ֭דַעְתָּ וְלֹ֣א נֵדָ֑ע תָּ֝בִ֗ין וְֽלֹא־עִמָּ֥נוּ הֽוּא׃
verses.king_james: What knowest thou, that we know not? what understandest thou, which is not in us?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et senes et antiqui sunt in nobis,  multo vetustiores quam patres tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־שָׂ֣ב גַּם־יָשִׁ֣ישׁ בָּ֑נוּ כַּבִּ֖יר מֵאָבִ֣יךָ יָמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: With us are both the grayheaded and very aged men, much elder than thy father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid grande est ut consoletur te Deus ?  sed verba tua prava hoc prohibent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַמְעַ֣ט מִ֭מְּךָ תַּנְחוּמ֣וֹת אֵ֑ל וְ֝דָבָ֗ר לָאַ֥ט עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Are the consolations of God small with thee? is there any secret thing with thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid te elevat cor tuum,  et quasi magna cogitans attonitos habes oculos ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־יִּקָּחֲךָ֥ לִבֶּ֑ךָ וּֽמַה־יִּרְזְמ֥וּן עֵינֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Why doth thine heart carry thee away? and what do thy eyes wink at,

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid tumet contra Deum spiritus tuus,  ut proferas de ore tuo hujuscemodi sermones ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־תָשִׁ֣יב אֶל־אֵ֣ל רוּחֶ֑ךָ וְהֹצֵ֖אתָ מִפִּ֣יךָ מִלִּֽין׃
verses.king_james: That thou turnest thy spirit against God, and lettest such words go out of thy mouth?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid est homo ut immaculatus sit,  et ut justus appareat natus de muliere ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מָה־אֱנ֥וֹשׁ כִּֽי־יִזְכֶּ֑ה וְכִי־יִ֝צְדַּ֗ק יְל֣וּד אִשָּֽׁה׃
verses.king_james: What is man, that he should be clean? and he which is born of a woman, that he should be righteous?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce inter sanctos ejus nemo immutabilis,  et cæli non sunt mundi in conspectu ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֣ן בִּ֭קְדֹשָׁו לֹ֣א יַאֲמִ֑ין וְ֝שָׁמַ֗יִם לֹא־זַכּ֥וּ בְעֵינָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: Behold, he putteth no trust in his saints; yea, the heavens are not clean in his sight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quanto magis abominabilis et inutilis homo,  qui bibit quasi aquam iniquitatem ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֭ף כִּי־נִתְעָ֥ב וְֽנֶאֱלָ֑ח אִישׁ־שֹׁתֶ֖ה כַמַּ֣יִם עַוְלָֽה׃
verses.king_james: How much more abominable and filthy is man, which drinketh iniquity like water?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ostendam tibi : audi me :  quod vidi, narrabo tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲחַוְךָ֥ שְֽׁמַֽע־לִ֑י וְזֶֽה־חָ֝זִ֗יתִי וַאֲסַפֵּֽרָה׃
verses.king_james: I will shew thee, hear me; and that which I have seen I will declare;

verses.latin_vulgate: Sapientes confitentur,  et non abscondunt patres suos :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁר־חֲכָמִ֥ים יַגִּ֑ידוּ וְלֹ֥א כִ֝חֲד֗וּ מֵאֲבוֹתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Which wise men have told from their fathers, and have not hid it:

verses.latin_vulgate: quibus solis data est terra,  et non transivit alienus per eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָהֶ֣ם לְ֭בַדָּם נִתְּנָ֣ה הָאָ֑רֶץ וְלֹא־עָ֖בַר זָ֣ר בְּתוֹכָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Unto whom alone the earth was given, and no stranger passed among them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cunctis diebus suis impius superbit,  et numerus annorum incertus est tyrannidis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־יְמֵ֣י רָ֭שָׁע ה֣וּא מִתְחוֹלֵ֑ל וּמִסְפַּ֥ר שָׁ֝נִ֗ים נִצְפְּנ֥וּ לֶעָרִֽיץ׃
verses.king_james: The wicked man travaileth with pain all his days, and the number of years is hidden to the oppressor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sonitus terroris semper in auribus illius :  et cum pax sit, ille semper insidias suspicatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קוֹל־פְּחָדִ֥ים בְּאׇזְנָ֑יו בַּ֝שָּׁל֗וֹם שׁוֹדֵ֥ד יְבוֹאֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: A dreadful sound is in his ears: in prosperity the destroyer shall come upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non credit quod reverti possit de tenebris ad lucem,  circumspectans undique gladium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־יַאֲמִ֣ין שׁ֭וּב מִנִּי־חֹ֑שֶׁךְ (וצפו) [וְצָפ֖וּי] ה֣וּא אֱלֵי־חָֽרֶב׃
verses.king_james: He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness, and he is waited for of the sword.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum se moverit ad quærendum panem,  novit quod paratus sit in manu ejus tenebrarum dies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֹ֘דֵ֤ד ה֣וּא לַלֶּ֣חֶם אַיֵּ֑ה יָדַ֓ע ׀ כִּי־נָכ֖וֹן בְּיָד֣וֹ יֽוֹם־חֹֽשֶׁךְ׃
verses.king_james: He wandereth abroad for bread, saying, Where is it? he knoweth that the day of darkness is ready at his hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Terrebit eum tribulatio,  et angustia vallabit eum,  sicut regem qui præparatur ad prælium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְֽ֭בַעֲתֻהוּ צַ֣ר וּמְצוּקָ֑ה תִּ֝תְקְפֵ֗הוּ כְּמֶ֤לֶךְ ׀ עָתִ֬יד לַכִּידֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: Trouble and anguish shall make him afraid; they shall prevail against him, as a king ready to the battle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tetendit enim adversus Deum manum suam,  et contra Omnipotentem roboratus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־נָטָ֣ה אֶל־אֵ֣ל יָד֑וֹ וְאֶל־שַׁ֝דַּ֗י יִתְגַּבָּֽר׃
verses.king_james: For he stretcheth out his hand against God, and strengtheneth himself against the Almighty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cucurrit adversus eum erecto collo,  et pingui cervice armatus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָר֣וּץ אֵלָ֣יו בְּצַוָּ֑אר בַּ֝עֲבִ֗י גַּבֵּ֥י מָגִנָּֽיו׃
verses.king_james: He runneth upon him, even on his neck, upon the thick bosses of his bucklers:

verses.latin_vulgate: Operuit faciem ejus crassitudo,  et de lateribus ejus arvina dependet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־כִסָּ֣ה פָנָ֣יו בְּחֶלְבּ֑וֹ וַיַּ֖עַשׂ פִּימָ֣ה עֲלֵי־כָֽסֶל׃
verses.king_james: Because he covereth his face with his fatness, and maketh collops of fat on his flanks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habitavit in civitatibus desolatis,  et in domibus desertis, quæ in tumulos sunt redactæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁכּ֤וֹן ׀ עָ֘רִ֤ים נִכְחָד֗וֹת בָּ֭תִּים לֹא־יֵ֣שְׁבוּ לָ֑מוֹ אֲשֶׁ֖ר הִתְעַתְּד֣וּ לְגַלִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: And he dwelleth in desolate cities, and in houses which no man inhabiteth, which are ready to become heaps.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non ditabitur, nec perseverabit substantia ejus,  nec mittet in terra radicem suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־יֶ֭עְשַׁר וְלֹא־יָק֣וּם חֵיל֑וֹ וְלֹא־יִטֶּ֖ה לָאָ֣רֶץ מִנְלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: He shall not be rich, neither shall his substance continue, neither shall he prolong the perfection thereof upon the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non recedet de tenebris :  ramos ejus arefaciet flamma,  et auferetur spiritu oris sui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־יָס֨וּר ׀ מִנִּי־חֹ֗שֶׁךְ יֹ֭נַקְתּוֹ תְּיַבֵּ֣שׁ שַׁלְהָ֑בֶת וְ֝יָס֗וּר בְּר֣וּחַ פִּֽיו׃
verses.king_james: He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches, and by the breath of his mouth shall he go away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non credet, frustra errore deceptus,  quod aliquo pretio redimendus sit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־יַאֲמֵ֣ן בַּשָּׁ֣ו נִתְעָ֑ה כִּי־שָׁ֝֗וְא תִּהְיֶ֥ה תְמוּרָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Let not him that is deceived trust in vanity: for vanity shall be his recompence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Antequam dies ejus impleantur peribit,  et manus ejus arescent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּֽלֹא־י֭וֹמוֹ תִּמָּלֵ֑א וְ֝כִפָּת֗וֹ לֹ֣א רַעֲנָֽנָה׃
verses.king_james: It shall be accomplished before his time, and his branch shall not be green.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lædetur quasi vinea in primo flore botrus ejus,  et quasi oliva projiciens florem suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַחְמֹ֣ס כַּגֶּ֣פֶן בִּסְר֑וֹ וְיַשְׁלֵ֥ךְ כַּ֝זַּ֗יִת נִצָּתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: He shall shake off his unripe grape as the vine, and shall cast off his flower as the olive.

verses.latin_vulgate: Congregatio enim hypocritæ sterilis,  et ignis devorabit tabernacula eorum qui munera libenter accipiunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־עֲדַ֣ת חָנֵ֣ף גַּלְמ֑וּד וְ֝אֵ֗שׁ אָכְלָ֥ה אׇהֳלֵי־שֹֽׁחַד׃
verses.king_james: For the congregation of hypocrites shall be desolate, and fire shall consume the tabernacles of bribery.

verses.latin_vulgate: Concepit dolorem, et peperit iniquitatem,  et uterus ejus præparat dolos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָרֹ֣ה עָ֭מָל וְיָ֣לֹֽד אָ֑וֶן וּ֝בִטְנָ֗ם תָּכִ֥ין מִרְמָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: They conceive mischief, and bring forth vanity, and their belly prepareth deceit.
